LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, (***PREVIOUSLY TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON 9/14/2020***) PLAINTIFF PALISADES COLLECTION, LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. JUDGMENT SHALL BE ENTERED FORTHWITH IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. A COURT CAN TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF INDISPUTABLE ADMISSIONS ON A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. (SEE COLUMBIA CASUALTY CO. V. NORTHWESTERN NAT. INS. CO. (1991) 231 CAL.APP.3D 457, 468-469; ARCE V. KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN INC. (2010) 181 CAL.APP.4TH 471, 485.) ON FEBRUARY 6, 2020, THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO DEEM PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS ADMITTED. DEFENDANT'S ADMISSIONS ESTABLISH PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS THAT DEFENDANT OWES THE DEBT AND PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT. COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE ADOPTED RULING OF THE COURT AND AN UPDATED PROPOSED JUDGMENT THAT REFLECTS ALL SUMS CURRENTLY OWED AND MUST EMAIL THEM TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. (302)

MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Plaintiff's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600 maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Off Calendar

Motion To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted
Off Calendar Feb-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF PALISADES COLLECTION,LLC's unopposed Motion To Deem Request For Admissions Admitted filed January 22, 2020 is denied as moot. It appears duplicative of Plaintiff's December 13, 2019 motion.

Judge Pro Tem: Maxwell Pritt; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted
Off Calendar Feb-06-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF PALISADES COLLECTION,LLC's unopposed Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted is granted. Under the Discovery Act, "the court is required to grant a deemed admission motion" where respondents fail to serve a verified response "before the hearing on that motion." (Tobin v. Oris (1992) 3 Cal.App.4th 814, 828, disapproved on other grounds of by Wilcox v. Birtwhistle (1999) 21 Cal.4th 973.) Defendant failed to serve a verified response and did not file an opposition to the Motion.

Plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge Pro Tem: Maxwell Pritt; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-03-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Defendant ROSCOE DUNCAN (Tel: 415-724-5612) appearing in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: Defendant Roscoe Duncan's Motion to Set Aside Default and Vacate Judgment.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed the parties to appear. Defendant ROSCOE DUNCAN is sworn and testifies. Matter submitted. The Court rules as follows: Defendant Roscoe Duncan's Motion to Set Aside Default and Vacate Judgment is granted. The Court finds no proof of service of the original Summons and Complaint. The Court finds the 2006 judgment and the renewal of that judgment in this case are void and set aside. The Court orders plaintiff to return to defendant the debited amount of $382.68 and the processing fee of $125.00. The Court will prepare and sign a formal order which it will then mail to the parties.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
